prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, the company's ledgers reveal a most wondrous increase in "Annual Recurring Revenue" by 37% unto $4.3 million, a sum sufficient to procure a small kingdom. In sooth, the "Customer Acquisition Cost" hath risen to $547, a figure most alarming, and yet the "Customer Lifetime Value" doth stretch forth unto $2,174, a boon most merciful. Furthermore, the "Churn Rate" doth stand at 12%, a plague upon the company's coffers, and yet the wise men of the company's council do claim that this is "within acceptable parameters". By the blessings of the spreadsheet saints, the "Gross Margin" hath expanded unto 85%, a marvel of alchemy that doth confound this humble chronicler.
